questions.stem_en: On a freeway with 3 or more lanes in your direction, the far left lane is generally used for:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在同方向有三條或更多車道的高速公路上，最左車道通常用於：
questions.explanation_en: On a freeway with 3 or more lanes, the far left lane is for passing or faster traffic, the middle for through traffic, and the far right for entering/exiting.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在同方向有三條或更多車道的高速公路上，最左車道供超車或快速車輛使用，中間車道供直行，最右車道用於進出。
questions.explanation_zh: 在同方向有三条或更多车道的高速公路上，最左车道供超车或快速车辆使用，中间车道供直行，最右车道用于进出。

questions.stem_en: When changing lanes, what is the correct procedure?
questions.stem_zhHant: 變道時，正確的程序是什麼？

options.text_en: Signal, change lanes, then check mirrors
options.text_zhHant: 打燈、變道、然後檢查後視鏡

options.text_en: Check mirrors, signal, then change lanes immediately
options.text_zhHant: 檢查後視鏡、打燈、然後立即變道

options.text_en: Signal, check mirrors, check blind spot, then change lanes when safe
options.text_zhHant: 打燈、檢查後視鏡、查看盲區、確認安全後變道

options.text_en: Honk your horn, then change lanes

questions.explanation_en: When changing lanes: signal, check mirrors, look over your shoulder to check your blind spot, and change lanes only when safe.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 變道時：先打轉向燈，檢查後視鏡，轉頭查看盲區，確認安全後再變道。
questions.explanation_zh: 变道时：先打转向灯，检查后视镜，转头查看盲区，确认安全后再变道。

questions.stem_en: A broken white line between lanes means:
questions.stem_zhHant: 車道之間的白色虛線表示：

options.text_en: You may change lanes when safe
The lane is for bicycles only
options.text_zhHant: 該車道僅供自行車使用

questions.explanation_en: A broken white line between same-direction lanes means you may change lanes when it is safe to do so.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 同方向車道之間的白色虛線表示在安全時可以變道。
questions.explanation_zh: 同方向车道之间的白色虚线表示在安全时可以变道。

questions.stem_en: Double solid yellow lines mean you may NOT cross them to pass, but you MAY cross them to:
questions.stem_zhHant: 雙實黃線表示不得越線超車，但可以越過它們來：

options.text_en: Turn left into a driveway or private road
options.text_zhHant: 左轉進入車道入口或私人道路

questions.explanation_en: You may cross double yellow lines to turn left into a driveway or private road, but not to pass another vehicle.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 可以越過雙實黃線左轉進入車道入口或私人道路，但不得用於超車。
questions.explanation_zh: 可以越过双实黄线左转进入车道入口或私人道路，但不得用于超车。

questions.stem_en: Frequent lane changes in traffic are dangerous because they:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在車流中頻繁變道很危險，因為：

options.text_en: Increase the risk of a collision

questions.explanation_en: Frequent lane changes (weaving) increase the risk of a collision. Do not weave in and out of traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 頻繁變道穿插會增加碰撞風險。不要在車流中頻繁變道。
questions.explanation_zh: 频繁变道穿插会增加碰撞风险。不要在车流中频繁变道。

questions.stem_en: Large broken white lines on the road indicate that:
questions.stem_zhHant: 路面上的大段白色虛線表示：

options.text_en: You are approaching a school zone
The lane you are in is ending and you must merge
options.text_zhHant: 您所在的車道即將結束，您必須匯入其他車道

options.text_en: You may park in this area

questions.explanation_en: Large broken white lines (wider and more closely spaced than normal lane markings) indicate that your lane is ending and you need to merge into the adjacent lane.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 大段白色虛線（比普通車道標線更寬且間距更近）表示您所在的車道即將結束，需要匯入相鄰車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 大段白色虚线（比普通车道标线更宽且间距更近）表示您所在的车道即将结束，需要汇入相邻车道。

questions.stem_en: What is the correct ORDER of steps when changing lanes?
questions.stem_zhHant: 變道時正確的步驟順序是什麼？

options.text_en: Check mirrors, change lanes, then signal
options.text_zhHant: 查看後視鏡、變道、然後打燈

options.text_en: Signal first, then check mirrors, then check blind spot over your shoulder
options.text_zhHant: 先打轉向燈，然後查看後視鏡，再轉頭查看盲區

options.text_en: Check blind spot, signal, then change lanes immediately
options.text_zhHant: 查看盲區、打燈、然後立即變道

options.text_en: Honk your horn, check mirrors, then change lanes
options.text_zhHant: 按喇叭、查看後視鏡、然後變道

questions.explanation_en: The correct order for changing lanes is: signal first, then check your mirrors, then look over your shoulder to check your blind spot, and change lanes only when it is safe.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 變道的正確順序是：先打轉向燈，然後查看後視鏡，再轉頭查看盲區，確認安全後才變道。
questions.explanation_zh: 变道的正确顺序是：先打转向灯，然后查看后视镜，再转头查看盲区，确认安全后才变道。

questions.stem_en: On a two-lane road, how many vehicles must be following you before you are required to use a turnout area to let them pass?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在雙車道道路上，有多少輛車跟在你後面時，你必須使用讓行區域讓他們通過？
questions.explanation_en: You must use a turnout area when 5 or more vehicles are following you on a two-lane road.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當雙車道道路上有5輛或更多車輛跟在你後面時，你必須使用讓行區域。
questions.explanation_zh: 当双车道道路上有5辆或更多车辆跟在你后面时，你必须使用让行区域。

questions.stem_en: When preparing to turn, you should begin signaling approximately how many feet before the turn?
questions.stem_zhHant: 準備轉彎時，應在轉彎前約多少英尺開始打轉向燈？
questions.explanation_en: Begin signaling about 100 feet before the turn.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在轉彎前約100英尺開始打轉向燈。
questions.explanation_zh: 在转弯前约100英尺开始打转向灯。

questions.stem_en: You may turn left on a red light ONLY when:
questions.stem_zhHant: 只有在以下哪種情況下可以在紅燈時左轉？

options.text_en: You are on a one-way street turning onto another one-way street
options.text_zhHant: 從單行道轉入另一條單行道

questions.explanation_en: You may turn left on red only from a one-way street onto another one-way street, after stopping and yielding.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 只有從單行道轉入另一條單行道時，才可以在紅燈時左轉。必須先停車並讓行。
questions.explanation_zh: 只有从单行道转入另一条单行道时，才可以在红灯时左转。必须先停车并让行。

questions.stem_en: When turning right, if there is a bike lane you should enter it no more than how many feet before the turn?
questions.stem_zhHant: 右轉時，如果有自行車道，應在轉彎前不超過多少英尺處駛入？
questions.explanation_en: When turning right, enter the bike lane no more than 200 feet before the turn.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 右轉時，在轉彎前不超過200英尺處駛入自行車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 右转时，在转弯前不超过200英尺处驶入自行车道。

questions.stem_en: If you are already in the intersection waiting to turn left and the light changes to red, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果您已經在路口中等待左轉且信號燈變紅，您應該：

options.text_en: Back up to behind the stop line
Wait for the next green light

questions.explanation_en: If you are already in the intersection and the light changes to red, complete your turn when safe.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果已經在路口中且信號燈變紅，在安全時完成轉彎。
questions.explanation_zh: 如果已经在路口中且信号灯变红，在安全时完成转弯。

questions.stem_en: A center left turn lane should be used for:
questions.stem_zhHant: 中央左轉車道應用於：

options.text_en: Preparing for and making a left turn or U-turn only
options.text_zhHant: 僅用於準備和執行左轉或掉頭

questions.explanation_en: A center left turn lane should only be used to prepare for and make a left turn or a permitted U-turn. Do not travel in this lane.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 中央左轉車道只能用於準備和執行左轉或允許的掉頭，不可在此車道內行駛。
questions.explanation_zh: 中央左转车道只能用于准备和执行左转或允许的掉头，不可在此车道内行驶。

questions.stem_en: When making a right turn on red, you must first:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在紅燈時右轉，您必須首先：

options.text_en: Slow down and check for traffic
Come to a complete stop and yield to pedestrians and traffic
options.text_zhHant: 完全停車並讓行人和車輛先行

options.text_en: Honk your horn to warn pedestrians

questions.explanation_en: You must come to a complete stop and yield to pedestrians and cross traffic before turning right on red.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 紅燈時右轉必須先完全停車，讓行人和橫向車輛先行。
questions.explanation_zh: 红灯时右转必须先完全停车，让行人和横向车辆先行。

questions.stem_en: When making a left turn from a two-way road, you should complete the turn into:
questions.stem_zhHant: 從雙向道路左轉時，您應該駛入目標道路的：

options.text_en: The lane closest to the center line of the road you enter
The lane with the least traffic

questions.explanation_en: Complete the turn in the lane closest to the center line of the road you enter.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駛入目標道路時進入最靠近中心線的車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 驶入目标道路时进入最靠近中心线的车道。

questions.stem_en: When turning right onto a road that has a dedicated right-turn lane (acceleration lane), you:
questions.stem_zhHant: 當右轉駛入一條設有專用右轉車道（加速車道）的道路時，您：

options.text_en: Must come to a complete stop before turning
options.text_zhHant: 必須完全停車後再轉彎

options.text_en: Can turn without stopping if you have a clear path into the dedicated lane
options.text_zhHant: 如果進入專用車道的路徑暢通，可以不停車直接轉入

options.text_en: Must wait for the traffic light to turn green

questions.explanation_en: When turning right onto a road with a dedicated right-turn lane, you can proceed into that lane without stopping, as long as the path is clear and it is safe to do so.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當右轉駛入設有專用右轉車道的道路時，只要路徑暢通且安全，可以不停車直接駛入該車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 当右转驶入设有专用右转车道的道路时，只要路径畅通且安全，可以不停车直接驶入该车道。

questions.stem_en: When making a left turn from a two-way street onto a one-way street, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 從雙向道路左轉進入單行道時，您應該：

options.text_en: Turn into any lane on the one-way street
options.text_zhHant: 轉入單行道的任何車道

options.text_en: Turn into the far left lane of the one-way street
options.text_zhHant: 轉入單行道最左側的車道

options.text_en: Turn into the far right lane of the one-way street
options.text_zhHant: 轉入單行道最右側的車道

questions.explanation_en: When turning left from a two-way street onto a one-way street, complete the turn into the far left lane of the one-way street.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 從雙向道路左轉進入單行道時，駛入單行道最左側的車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 从双向道路左转进入单行道时，驶入单行道最左侧的车道。

questions.stem_en: U-turns are illegal in which of the following situations?
questions.stem_zhHant: 以下哪種情況下掉頭是違法的？

options.text_en: At an intersection with a green light
In a residential area with no traffic within 200 feet
options.text_zhHant: 在200英尺內無車輛的住宅區

options.text_en: At an intersection with a green arrow

questions.explanation_en: U-turns are illegal on one-way streets, across double yellow lines, on divided highways, and where a No U-Turn sign is posted.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在單行道上、越過雙黃線、在分隔公路上以及有禁止掉頭標誌的地方掉頭都是違法的。
questions.explanation_zh: 在单行道上、越过双黄线、在分隔公路上以及有禁止掉头标志的地方掉头都是违法的。

questions.stem_en: On a highway, a U-turn is illegal if other drivers cannot see you within:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在公路上，如果其他駕駛員在多少英尺內無法看到您，則掉頭違法？
questions.explanation_en: U-turns are illegal on a highway where you cannot be seen by other drivers within 200 feet in either direction.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果在公路上任一方向200英尺內其他駕駛員無法看到你，則掉頭違法。
questions.explanation_zh: 如果在公路上任一方向200英尺内其他驾驶员无法看到你，则掉头违法。

questions.stem_en: U-turns are NOT allowed in front of:
questions.stem_zhHant: 以下哪處前方不允許掉頭？
questions.explanation_en: U-turns are not allowed in front of fire stations. Never use a fire station driveway to turn around.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 消防站前方不允許掉頭。永遠不要使用消防站的車道來掉頭。
questions.explanation_zh: 消防站前方不允许掉头。永远不要使用消防站的车道来掉头。

questions.stem_en: In a residential area, a U-turn is legal when:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在住宅區，以下哪種情況下掉頭合法？

options.text_en: There is a No U-Turn sign
No vehicle is approaching within 200 feet
You are on a divided highway

questions.explanation_en: In a residential area, a U-turn is legal when no vehicle is approaching within 200 feet.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在住宅區，當200英尺內沒有車輛接近時，掉頭是合法的。
questions.explanation_zh: 在住宅区，当200英尺内没有车辆接近时，掉头是合法的。

questions.stem_en: You should never make a U-turn near the top of a hill because:
questions.stem_zhHant: 不應在山頂附近掉頭，因為：

options.text_en: It is hard on your brakes
Other drivers cannot see you from at least 200 feet away
options.text_zhHant: 其他駕駛員在200英尺內看不到你

questions.explanation_en: Never make a U-turn near the top of a hill or on a curve where other drivers cannot see you from at least 200 feet away.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 不要在山頂附近或彎道處掉頭，因為其他駕駛員在200英尺內可能看不到你。
questions.explanation_zh: 不要在山顶附近或弯道处掉头，因为其他驾驶员在200英尺内可能看不到你。

questions.stem_en: When may you pass on the right side of another vehicle?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候可以從右側超車？

options.text_en: Whenever you want to go faster
When the vehicle ahead is making a left turn, or on a one-way street
options.text_zhHant: 當前車正在左轉，或在單行道上

options.text_en: When there is a solid yellow line

questions.explanation_en: You may pass on the right only when the vehicle ahead is making a left turn, on a one-way street, or on a road with 2+ lanes in your direction.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 只有在前車正在左轉、在單行道上或在同方向有兩條以上車道的道路上，才可以從右側超車。
questions.explanation_zh: 只有在前车正在左转、在单行道上或在同方向有两条以上车道的道路上，才可以从右侧超车。

questions.stem_en: You should NOT pass another vehicle within how many feet of an intersection?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在距離路口多少英尺以內不應超車？
questions.explanation_en: Do not pass within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在距離路口或鐵路道口100英尺內不要超車。
questions.explanation_zh: 在距离路口或铁路道口100英尺内不要超车。

questions.stem_en: Before returning to the right lane after passing, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 超車後返回右車道之前，您應該：

options.text_en: Wait until you can see the passed vehicle's front in your rearview mirror
options.text_zhHant: 等到能在後視鏡中看到被超車輛的前部

options.text_en: Flash your headlights at the passed vehicle

questions.explanation_en: Before returning to the right lane, make sure you can see the front of the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 超車後返回右車道之前，確保能在後視鏡中看到被超車輛的前部。
questions.explanation_zh: 超车后返回右车道之前，确保能在后视镜中看到被超车辆的前部。

questions.stem_en: When being passed by another vehicle, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 被其他車輛超車時，您應該：

options.text_en: Speed up to prevent the pass
Maintain your speed or slow down
Honk your horn as a warning

questions.explanation_en: When being passed, maintain your speed or slow down. Do not speed up to prevent the pass.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 被超車時，保持車速或減速。不要加速阻止超車。
questions.explanation_zh: 被超车时，保持车速或减速。不要加速阻止超车。

questions.stem_en: It is illegal to pass on a two-lane road when:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在雙車道道路上，以下哪種情況超車違法？

options.text_en: There is a broken yellow line on your side
There is a solid yellow line on your side
The road is straight and flat
You can see oncoming traffic clearly

questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to pass on a two-lane road when a solid yellow line is on your side of the center line.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在雙車道道路上，如果中心線在你這一側是實黃線，則超車違法。
questions.explanation_zh: 在双车道道路上，如果中心线在你这一侧是实黄线，则超车违法。

questions.stem_en: HOV (carpool) lanes are marked with a:
questions.stem_zhHant: HOV（合乘）車道用什麼標記？
questions.explanation_en: HOV/carpool lanes are marked with a diamond symbol.
questions.explanation_zhHant: HOV/合乘車道用菱形標誌標記。
questions.explanation_zh: HOV/合乘车道用菱形标志标记。

questions.stem_en: Which vehicles may use HOV lanes regardless of the number of occupants?
questions.stem_zhHant: 哪些車輛無論車內人數都可以使用HOV車道？

options.text_en: Motorcycles, buses, and clean air vehicles with special decals
options.text_zhHant: 摩托車、公交車和持有特殊貼紙的清潔能源車輛

questions.explanation_en: Motorcycles, buses, and certain clean air vehicles with special DMV decals may use HOV lanes regardless of occupants.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 摩托車、公交車以及持有特殊DMV貼紙的清潔能源車輛無論車內人數都可以使用HOV車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 摩托车、公交车以及持有特殊DMV贴纸的清洁能源车辆无论车内人数都可以使用HOV车道。

questions.stem_en: You may enter or exit an HOV lane separated by double solid white lines:
questions.stem_zhHant: 以雙白實線分隔的HOV車道，您可以在哪裡進出？

options.text_en: Whenever there is a gap in traffic

questions.explanation_en: You may only cross double solid white lines to enter or exit an HOV lane at designated entry/exit points.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 只能在指定的進出口處越過雙白實線進入或離開HOV車道。
questions.explanation_zh: 只能在指定的进出口处越过双白实线进入或离开HOV车道。

questions.stem_en: A lane marked with a red X overhead means:
questions.stem_zhHant: 上方標有紅色X的車道表示：

options.text_en: The lane is for emergency vehicles only
options.text_zhHant: 該車道僅供緊急車輛使用

options.text_en: Do not drive in this lane
The lane is for turning only

questions.explanation_en: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X. It means the lane is closed to traffic in your direction.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 絕不要在標有紅色X的車道行駛。這表示該車道對你的行駛方向關閉。
questions.explanation_zh: 绝不要在标有红色X的车道行驶。这表示该车道对你的行驶方向关闭。

questions.stem_en: You should not drive in a bike lane except when:
questions.stem_zhHant: 除了以下哪種情況外，不應在自行車道內行駛？

options.text_en: Making a turn within 200 feet of the turn
options.text_zhHant: 在距轉彎200英尺內準備轉彎

questions.explanation_en: Do not drive in a bike lane except when making a turn within 200 feet of the turn.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 除非在距轉彎200英尺內準備轉彎，否則不得在自行車道內行駛。
questions.explanation_zh: 除非在距转弯200英尺内准备转弯，否则不得在自行车道内行驶。

questions.stem_en: Under what condition may you drive through a public transit bus lane?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在什麼情況下你可以駛過公車專用道？

options.text_en: When the bus lane is empty
When driving below the speed limit

questions.explanation_en: You may cross a bus lane to make a right turn, but you cannot drive, stop, or park in a bus-only lane.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 你可以穿過公車道進行右轉，但不能在公車專用道上行駛、停車或泊車。
questions.explanation_zh: 你可以穿过公车道进行右转，但不能在公车专用道上行驶、停车或泊车。

questions.stem_en: When merging onto a highway from an on-ramp, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 從匝道匯入高速公路時，您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait for a large gap
options.text_zhHant: 在匝道末端停車等待大的間隙

options.text_en: Match the speed of highway traffic and merge smoothly; do not stop on the ramp
options.text_zhHant: 配合高速公路車流速度順暢匯入，不要在匝道上停車

options.text_en: Drive slowly and force other vehicles to make room
options.text_zhHant: 慢速行駛並迫使其他車輛讓路

options.text_en: Turn on your hazard lights while merging

questions.explanation_en: When merging onto a highway, use the on-ramp to match the speed of highway traffic and merge smoothly. Do not stop on the ramp unless traffic ahead has stopped.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 匯入高速公路時，利用匝道加速至與高速公路車流相匹配的速度，順暢匯入。除非前方車流已停止，否則不要在匝道上停車。
questions.explanation_zh: 汇入高速公路时，利用匝道加速至与高速公路车流相匹配的速度，顺畅汇入。除非前方车流已停止，否则不要在匝道上停车。

questions.stem_en: When exiting a highway, how far in advance should you signal?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駛出高速公路時，應提前多遠打轉向燈？

options.text_en: At least 100 feet before the exit
Signal about 5 seconds before changing lanes to the exit
options.text_zhHant: 在變道駛向出口前約5秒鐘打燈

options.text_en: Just as you reach the exit ramp
options.text_zhHant: 到達出口匝道時才打燈

options.text_en: No signal is necessary on the highway
options.text_zhHant: 在高速公路上不需要打燈

questions.explanation_en: When exiting a highway, signal approximately 5 seconds before changing lanes to move toward the exit. This gives other drivers enough time to react.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駛出高速公路時，在變道駛向出口前約5秒鐘打轉向燈，讓其他駕駛員有足夠時間反應。
questions.explanation_zh: 驶出高速公路时，在变道驶向出口前约5秒钟打转向灯，让其他驾驶员有足够时间反应。

questions.stem_en: If you miss your freeway exit, what should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果您錯過了高速公路出口，您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Stop and back up to the exit
Make a U-turn on the freeway

options.text_zhHant: 繼續行駛到下一個出口

options.text_en: Cross the median to turn around

questions.explanation_en: If you miss your freeway exit, do not stop, back up, or try to cross the median. Continue to the next exit.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果錯過高速公路出口，不要停車、倒車或試圖穿越中央隔離帶。繼續行駛到下一個出口。
questions.explanation_zh: 如果错过高速公路出口，不要停车、倒车或试图穿越中央隔离带。继续行驶到下一个出口。

questions.stem_en: When exiting the freeway, when should you begin to slow down?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駛出高速公路時，什麼時候應該開始減速？

options.text_en: While still in the travel lanes
Only after you are in the exit lane or deceleration lane
options.text_zhHant: 只有進入出口車道或減速車道後

options.text_en: Right at the exit ramp curve

questions.explanation_en: Do not slow down until you are in the exit lane or deceleration lane. Slowing down in the travel lanes can cause rear-end collisions.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在進入出口車道或減速車道之前不要減速。在行車道上減速可能導致追尾碰撞。
questions.explanation_zh: 在进入出口车道或减速车道之前不要减速。在行车道上减速可能导致追尾碰撞。

questions.stem_en: Should you stop on the freeway on-ramp before merging?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在高速公路入口匝道上應該在匯入前停車嗎？

options.text_en: Yes, always stop to check for traffic
options.text_zhHant: 是的，總是停車檢查車流

options.text_en: No, unless traffic is already stopped
Yes, stop and wait for a large gap

questions.explanation_en: Do not stop on the freeway on-ramp unless traffic is already stopped. Merge into traffic at or near the speed of traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 除非車流已經停止，否則不要在高速公路入口匝道上停車。以接近車流速度匯入。
questions.explanation_zh: 除非车流已经停止，否则不要在高速公路入口匝道上停车。以接近车流速度汇入。
